title: Currency Ordering and Precious Metals
body_text:
Currency Ordering and Precious Metals
1	Introduction to Currency Ordering and Precious Metals
1.1	Overview of Currency Ordering
This Part describes the processes associated with ordering and returning currency and provides procedures on how to use the Cash Services system.
Cash Services allows financial institutions to order Canadian and US bank notes and Canadian coin. This application is available through a partnership between Central 1 and Royal Bank of Canada (RBC). Central 1 administers the set-up of financial institutions with RBC. Once established, financial institutions manage their locations, currency limits, and Cash Services Users.
Authorized users access Cash Services through Central 1âs Client Centre. User management for the Client Centre is managed through User Management or CPS Admin. See the User Management Part or the CPS Admin Application Part, as applicable, for procedures on creating, modifying, and deleting Client Centre Users and Admin Security Officers.
Note:	Currently, there are two user management applications running parallel: CPS Admin and User Management. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin. CPS Admin Security Officers are encouraged to use the User Management application.
Cash Services is available any time, seven days a week. Users can create one order per location per delivery date. The order placement cut-off time is 11:00 am local time. Orders placed after 11:00 am will have an extra day added before the order can be dispatched.
Admin Users can generate user audit reports and reports of previous orders for locations they are assigned to. See Chapter 8, Generating Reports in Cash Services.
Financial institutions arrange delivery routes with the armoured car company. The armoured car company picks up the currency on the dispatch date and delivers it to the appropriate location (branch or ATM) on the delivery date.
This Part also describes the processes associated with purchasing and buying back precious metals. See Section 14.1, Overview of Precious Metals, for more information.

Recap sheets and waybills
ACC
ACC:
Order recap sheets and waybills from your armoured car company.
D+H:
Reorder CAD and US deposit slips from Davis & Henderson (D+H). If you donât have reorder forms from D+H, contact RBC.
Note:	Deposit slips are used to return currency. Central 1 will arrange delivery for the initial batch of Canadian and US deposit slips for newly opened financial institution locations. The deposit slip printing company used is Davis and Henderson (D+H).
A D+H deposit slip reorder form is included in every package of deposit slips. Please retain this form and use it for subsequent deposit slip reorders. Reorder instructions are provided on the D+H reorder forms.
New deposit slip orders and reorders are delivered within 10â15 days.
END
